audiences.body: Use the DALG manifest, report figures, and metrics snapshots to inspect the structural contribution rather than only the final RGB reconstruction.
audiences.title: Researchers

audiences.body: Use the local web UI to upload an image, run a decomposition mode, inspect grouped layers, and export a canonical design manifest without working directly from the CLI.
audiences.title: Editors and art directors

audiences.body: Use the Python CLI and the canonical DALG export as the integration surface for external tools, Qwen enrichment, prompt extraction, and benchmark automation.
audiences.title: Developers

effects.note: The associated-effect extractor is a heuristic prototype rather than a trained visual-effects decomposer.

prompt_extract.note: Point-only and box-only prompts localize geometry well but do not satisfy the semantic hit criterion without text guidance.

transparent.note: Transparent recomposition is treated as a sanity check. Alpha quality and clean-background quality remain the primary transparent-layer metrics.

comparisons.description: Full native graph pipeline with semantic grouping, amodal completion, intrinsics, and ordered RGBA export.
comparisons.label: LayerForge native

comparisons.description: Raw Qwen layers with DALG metadata while preserving the best external visual stack.
comparisons.label: Qwen + graph preserve (4)

comparisons.description: Raw Qwen layers re-exported through the LayerForge depth graph for graph-ordered layering.
comparisons.label: Qwen + graph reorder (4)

comparisons.description: Graph-guided recursive peeling with residual inpainting and explicit front-to-back layer logging.
comparisons.label: LayerForge peeling

comparisons.description: Direct Qwen-Image-Layered RGBA decomposition used as the frontier generative baseline.
comparisons.label: Qwen raw (4)

contributions: Depth-Aware Amodal Layer Graph (DALG) export as the canonical scene representation.
Native layered decomposition with semantic grouping, occlusion ordering, and optional per-layer intrinsics.
Qwen-Image-Layered import and graph enrichment in preserve-order and reorder modes.
Recursive semantic peeling, prompt-conditioned extraction, transparent-layer recovery, and effect-layer prototyping.
Submission-safe evidence pack with measured JSON snapshots, figures, and a reproducible report build path.

modes.description: Run the native LayerForge pipeline and inspect ordered layers, contact sheets, and recomposition.
modes.label: Layered decomposition

modes.description: Run the decomposition and export one selected editable target layer plus background-completed previews.
modes.label: Prompt extraction

modes.description: Approximate a transparent or semi-transparent foreground and estimate a clean background.
modes.label: Transparent target recovery

modes.description: Iteratively peel the front-most editable layer while logging the residual canvas.
modes.label: Recursive peeling

project.abstract: LayerForge-X represents a single RGB image as a depth-aware amodal layer graph (DALG) whose nodes are editable RGBA layers and whose edges encode pairwise occlusion evidence. The repository couples a native graph pipeline with Qwen-Image-Layered enrichment, recursive peeling, prompt-conditioned extraction, transparent-layer recovery, and editability-aware evaluation.
project.name: LayerForge-X-final
project.tagline: Depth-aware amodal layered image decomposition from a single RGB image
